name: Cranberry-Jalapeno Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the berries and discard any that are brown and soft.
2. Finely grate the zest of the orange peel.
3. Cut the orange in half and squeeze out the juice. Add enough water to the orange juice to make one cup of liquid.
4. Combine the berries, grated orange peel, orange juice with water, tequila, and sugar in a large non-reactive pot. Bring it to a slow boil, stirring occasionally as the sugar dissolves.
5. Trim the jalapeno, discard the seeds, and chop it finely.
6. When the berries begin to pop, add the chopped jalapeno and continue boiling everything together for about 5 more minutes, stirring with a long wooden spoon.
7. If the sauce seems too thick, add a little more water.
8. Remove the sauce from the heat and allow it to cool, stirring occasionally to prevent a skin from forming. Spoon into a pretty dish and chill if desired.
This delicious, slightly spicy sauce is perfect alongside tamales, with cheese-stuffed crepes, or any similarly rich-flavored dish, or even with your Thanksgiving turkey.
